content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 22,
1992
R.
Jane Vernon
30268 Mersey Court
Temecula,
CA 92591
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1-92-575
Dear Ms.
Vernon:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice
regarding your duties as a potential city councilmember under the
conflict—of—interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”).
1
Since your advice request does not refer to a specific
governmental decision,
but instead seeks general guidance,
we are
treating your request as one for informal assistance.
2
QUESTION
If you are elected to the Temecula City Council,
would your
ownership interest in Kemper Financial mutual funds prevent you
from participating in city council decisions concerning land use
by the Kemper Real Estate Management Company?
CONCLUS I ON
Assuming that Kemper Financial is
a diversified mutual
fund
registered with the Securities and Exchange Commission and that
your interest does not exceed
10% of the total assets of the fund,
Kemper Financial does not constitute an “investment interest.”
Thus,
absent some other disqualifying economic interest as set
forth in Section 87103, it appears that you would be able to
participate in such city council decisions.
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3).)
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-92-575
Page
2
FACTS
You are a candidate for city council,
City of Temecula.
You
own Kemper Financial mutual funds.
Additionally,
you control such
funds in an irrevocable trust for your mother,
Martha Adams,
a
resident of West Palm Beach,
Florida.
The original development project for the City of Temecula was
owned by Kaiser Development.
Kaiser Development sold the land to
Bedford Properties.
Bedford Properties has a joint arrangement
with the Kemper Real Estate Management Company.
Recently, the
Kemper Real Estate Management Company took exclusive control of
the remaining unsold and undeveloped land,
as well as some retail
centers.
During our telephone conversation of August 10,
1992,
you advised me that the Kemper Real Estate Management Company is
an affiliate of Kemper Financial mutual funds.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her official
position to influence a governmental decision in which the
official has a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies that
an official has a financial interest if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial
effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public generally,
on the official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1,000)
or more.
Section 87103.
Section 82034 defines an “investment”
That section
provides:
“Investment” means any financial interest in
or security issued by a business entity,
including
but not limited to common stock, preferred stock,
rights, warrants, options,
debt instruments and any
partnership or other ownership interest owned
directly,
indirectly or beneficially by the public
official,
or other filer,
or his or her immediate
family, if the business entity or any parent,
subsidiary or otherwise related business entity has
an interest in real property in the jurisdiction,
or does business or plans to do business in the
jurisdiction,
or has done business within the
jurisdiction at any time during the two years prior
to the time any statement or other action is


Our File No.
A-92-575
Page
3
required under this title.
No asset shall be
deemed an investment unless its fair market value
equals or exceeds one thousand dollars
($1,000).
The term “investment” does not include
a time or
demand deposit in a financial institution,
shares
in a credit union,
any insurance policy,
interest
in a diversified mutual fund registered with the
Securities and Exchange Commission under the
Investment Company Act of 1940 or a common trust
fund which is created pursuant to Section 1564 of
the Financial Code,
or any bond or other debt
instrument issued by any government or government
agency.
Investments of an individual includes a
pro rata share of investments of any business
entity,
mutual fund,
or trust in which the
individual or immediate family owns, directly,
indirectly or beneficially,
a ro-percent interest
or greater.
3
The term “parent,
subsidiary or
otherwise related business entity” shall be
specifically defined by regulations of the
commission.
Emphasis added.
In addition, Regulation 18706 provides that an official has a
financial interest in a decision within the meaning of Section
87100 if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have
a material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on
the public generally,
on a business entity which is a parent or
subsidiary of,
or is otherwise related to,
a business entity in
which the official has a financial interest.
(See, Regulation
18236.)
The Kemper Real Estate Management Company is an affiliate of
Kemper Financial mutual funds.
Thus,
assuming that Kemper
Financial is a diversified mutual fund registered with the
Securities and Exchange Commission under the Investment Company
Act of 1940 and that your interest in Kemper Financial mutual
funds does not exceed
10% of the total assets of the fund,
you do
not have an “investment” interest in Kemper Financial mutual funds
as that term is defined in the Act.
If you have a direct,
indirect or beneficial interest in your mother’s trust, the same
analysis would apply to the assets held by your mother in Kemper
Financial mutual funds.
Consequently,
absent some other disqualifying economic
interest as set forth in Section 87103, it appears that you would
be able to participate in such city council decisions.
“Immediate family” means the spouse and dependent children.
(Section 82029.)


Our File No.
A-92-575
Page
4
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested.
If you have any further questions regarding this
matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—59Ol.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
A9t4Ldt
By:
Deanne Stone
Counsel,
Legal Division
SH:DRS:aa
“
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of
lOc per page.

would your

from participating in city council decisions concerning land use
by the Kemper Real Estate Management Company?

Kemper Financial does not constitute an “investment interest.”
Thus,

participate in such city council decisions.
